summary: Fix browser-side timesheet id allocation, make timesheet approval generation data-driven, and improve approval-chain readability.

notes: timesheets.id is owned by public.timesheets_id_seq and the sequence is kept ahead of existing rows.
Timesheet business type inference, template routing, runtime node expansion, role aliases, and missing-assignee behavior are stored in database configuration.
submit_document now delegates template selection to psa_select_approval_template and graph expansion to psa_expand_approval_template.
Internal approval helpers remain ungranted to authenticated users.
Timesheet creation no longer sends an explicit id from the browser.
Concurrent same-user same-period timesheet creation retries by reading the existing unique user/week row.
Approval-chain cards show template node key, role, project details, node status, assignee status, action, comments, and time.
Long approval chains scroll inside the chain panel instead of widening the whole page.

verification: Run migration rollback canary for 101 plus a transaction-only submit.
After deploy, run deploy/scripts/pre-deploy-check.sh with EXPECTED_VERSION=V0.18.0.
After deploy, run scripts/smoke-frontend-render.mjs against https://xpjs.asia/.
known_warnings: ESLint still reports existing warnings in dashboard/ui/TimesheetPage files, but exits 0.
Vite still warns that the main index chunk is larger than 500 kB.
